metaTitle: Egress Window Installation in Hickman, NE | Lincoln Egress
metaDescription: Egress window installation and window well service for Hickman, NE basements. Licensed, insured, and offering free written estimates. Call (509) 351-8404 now.
h1: Egress Window Installation in Hickman, NE
heroSub: Hickman is one of the fastest-growing communities in Lancaster County, and a lot of that growth means big unfinished basements waiting for a legal bedroom. We install code-compliant egress windows for Hickman homeowners and back every job with a free written estimate.
introHtml:
Hickman has grown fast over the last two decades, and most of that growth shows up as newer construction in the Norris School District draw area with full, unfinished basements. Those basements are a great head start on a finished bedroom or living space, but Nebraska code still requires a proper egress window before that space can legally count as a bedroom.

We work with homeowners throughout Hickman on new egress window installation, egress window replacement, and window well installation. Every job starts with a free written estimate, and we're licensed, insured, and available 24/7 for inspection deadlines and closing timelines.

services.title: Egress Window Installation
services.desc: Full cut-in service for Hickman's newer homes with large unfinished basements, so that extra square footage can become a legal bedroom or family room.

services.title: Egress Window Replacement
services.desc: Swapping out an undersized or aging basement window for a code-compliant egress unit, common in Hickman homes built before the city's biggest growth years.

services.title: Window Well Installation
services.desc: Properly sized wells with drainage built for Lancaster County soil and weather, keeping newer Hickman basements dry through spring and summer storms.

services.title: Basement Window Replacement
services.desc: Upgrading standard basement windows to egress-ready openings as growing Hickman families finish out basement space for kids' bedrooms or guest rooms.

sections.heading: Why Egress Windows Matter for Hickman's New Construction
sections.html:
Hickman has been one of the fastest-growing small cities in Lancaster County, and most of that growth is homes built in the 2000s and later with big, open basements. Builders often leave that space unfinished, which means the egress window may not have been part of the original build if the basement wasn't planned as living space from day one.

If you're finishing that basement now for a bedroom, home office, or extra living area, an egress window isn't optional. It's the difference between a room that's just storage and one that's a legal, sellable bedroom.

We handle the full egress window installation process for Hickman homes, from the initial cut to a finished, code-compliant opening.

sections.heading: Serving Hickman and the Norris School District Area
sections.html:
Hickman sits just south of Lincoln and draws families in for the Norris School District, which has pulled a steady stream of new residential construction into the area. That growth has made Hickman one of the more active markets in Lancaster County for basement finishing projects.

We're familiar with the mix of home styles across Hickman, from newer subdivisions to older homes closer to the city's historic core, and we size every egress window and window well to the specific home rather than a one-size-fits-all approach.

Whether you're in a brand-new build or an older Hickman home, we can walk your basement and give you a clear picture of what a compliant egress opening will take. See our egress window cost page for typical pricing ranges.

sections.heading: Window Wells Built for Nebraska Weather
sections.html:
A new egress window is only as good as the window well around it. Poor drainage or an undersized well leads to water sitting against the foundation, which is a real risk during Nebraska's heavier spring rains.

We build every window well to drain properly and hold up to freeze-thaw cycles, so Hickman homeowners aren't dealing with a flooded well or a cracked cover a few years down the road.

If you're not sure whether your current window well is doing its job, we're happy to take a look and give you a straight answer, no pressure.

faq.q: Do older homes in Hickman need egress windows too, or is this just for new construction?
faq.a: Any basement being finished as a bedroom needs a compliant egress window, regardless of when the home was built. Hickman has a mix of older homes near the city center and newer construction from the recent growth years, and we install and retrofit egress windows in both.

faq.q: How long does egress window installation take for a typical Hickman basement?
faq.a: Most single-window installations take one to two days depending on foundation type and whether we're cutting into poured concrete or block. Newer Hickman homes with standard basement layouts are usually on the faster end, while older foundations can take a bit longer.

faq.q: Will an egress window help if I'm planning to sell my Hickman home?
faq.a: Yes. A basement bedroom without a legal egress window can hold up a sale or come up as a red flag during inspection. Given how many buyers are looking at Hickman right now, having that basement bedroom already code-compliant is a real advantage.

faq.q: Can you match the egress window to my home's existing style?
faq.a: Yes, we install a range of egress window styles and frame materials, and we'll help you pick an option that fits your home's exterior. We'll also confirm the well cover and grading work with your yard's slope, which varies across Hickman's different neighborhoods.
